name: CLI Commands
description: Implement every CLI command the spec promises, using the CLI foundation from Sprint 6 (including the task_77 visual identity module). Every command's output MUST match the product spec §6 verbatim — banner with mark, symbol vocabulary, kv-aligned layout, specific copy. Waves are ordered by dependency weight: simple read-only commands first, the heavyweight `run`/`resume` pair next, then the catalog-touching commands (install/search/upgrade).

waves.name: relay doctor
waves.description: Create `packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts`. Output MUST match product spec §6.2 verbatim. Header: `●─▶●─▶●─▶●  relay doctor`. Blank line. Five kv rows with SYMBOLS.ok/fail prefix: `node    <version>  (≥ 20.10 required)`, `claude  <version> at <path>`, `auth    <billingSource> (<detail>) via <source>` (e.g. 'subscription (max) via CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN'), `env     no conflicting ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` OR the blocking message from §6.2 when set, `dir     ./.relay writable`. When env check fails, render the three-line remediation from §6.2: `fix:`/`permanent:`/`override:` each with the exact command. Final line: `ready to run.` (green) or `<N> blocker(s) before you can run.` (red). Exit code 0 on pass, 3 on ANTHROPIC_API_KEY blocker (ClaudeAuthError code), 1 on other failures. CI-friendly: respect NO_COLOR and non-TTY. Delegate auth detection to ClaudeProvider.authenticate(). Import mark + symbols from task_77.

waves.name: relay runs
waves.description: Create `packages/cli/src/commands/runs.ts`. Lists past runs in `<cwd>/.relay/runs/`. Header: `●─▶●─▶●─▶●  recent runs`. Then an aligned table whose columns use the product-spec symbol vocabulary: status symbol (SYMBOLS.ok/fail for succeeded/failed, SYMBOLS.cancelled '⊘' for aborted, SYMBOLS.spinner '⠋' for running), runId, flowName v<ver>, startedAt relative (e.g. '2h ago'), duration. Read `state.json` per run dir. Sort by startedAt desc. Support `--limit N` (default 20) and `--status <s>` filter. Footer: one-line hint `resume any: relay resume <runId>`. Import visuals from task_77.

waves.name: relay list
waves.description: Create `packages/cli/src/commands/list.ts`. Output matches product spec §6.8 `relay list` example: header `●─▶●─▶●─▶●  installed flows (./.relay/flows/)`, then one aligned row per flow: `<name>    v<ver>    <duration>  $<cost>   <description>`. Fetch flows from local (`./.relay/flows/*/package.json`), workspace (`./node_modules/@ganderbite/flow-*/package.json`), and remote catalog (lazy fetch with 5s timeout; on network failure skip remote and print a gray note). Extract displayName/cost/duration from the `relay` package.json metadata block. Footer: `<N> flows installed. search more: relay search <query>`. Import visuals from task_77.

waves.name: relay run
waves.description: Create `packages/cli/src/commands/run.ts`. Implements the headline command. Flow: (1) `loadFlow(nameOrPath, cwd)` from task_44 — if not found locally and the name matches a catalog entry, auto-install first; (2) parse remaining argv via `parseInputFromArgv(flow.input, argv)`; (3) construct a Runner (providers: defaultRegistry, runDir: `./.relay/runs/<runId>`, logger with file + console=false so ProgressDisplay owns stdout); (4) call `ClaudeProvider.authenticate()` via runner and print `renderStartBanner` (task_47) per product spec §6.3 — MUST include the `bill` line and `press ctrl-c any time — state is saved after every step.` gray line; (5) start `ProgressDisplay` (task_48); (6) `await runner.run(flow, input)`; (7) stop ProgressDisplay, then call `renderSuccessBanner` or `renderFailureBanner` from task_47 based on result.status (product spec §6.5 and §6.6 — names specific 'next:' actions, names resume command verbatim on failure, always reports cost-so-far even on failure). (8) Exit code via exitCodeFor(err). Flags: `--resume <runId>` (delegates to resume cmd), `--cost` (prints per-step table), `--fresh` (delete any stale `./.relay/runs/<runId>` with same input-hash before starting — implemented in task_88 in Sprint 12), `--api-key` (sets runner.allowApiKey() — opt-in API billing from §6.2 remediation). Import visuals from task_77.

waves.name: relay resume
waves.description: Create `packages/cli/src/commands/resume.ts`. Output MUST match product spec §6.7 verbatim. Header: `●─▶●─▶●─▶●  relay resume <runId>`. Blank line. Kv rows `flow     <name> v<ver>`, `picking up from: <stepId>` (the earliest pending step). Blank line. Step grid where already-succeeded steps show `✓ <name>   (cached, ran <HH:MM>)` — the word 'cached' is intentional per §6.7 (stronger than 'skipped' — reassures user prior work counts). Currently running step shows spinner. Pending downstream steps show `○ <name>   waiting on X, Y`. Footer: `spent so far: $<prior>  ·  resume cost est: $<remaining>`. Read `<runDir>/flow-ref.json` to find the flow dir, re-load via `loadFlow`, construct a Runner, call `runner.resume(runDir)`. Reuse banner/progress from tasks 47+48. On state.json missing: `✕ no resumable run at <runId>` + 'did you mean: relay runs' hint, exit 1. Import visuals from task_77.

waves.name: relay install
waves.description: Create `packages/cli/src/commands/install.ts`. Accepts `<flow>[@<version>]`. Output matches product spec §6.8 `relay install` example: header `●─▶●─▶●─▶●  installing <flow>`, blank line, four SYMBOLS.ok rows as work progresses (`resolved @ganderbite/relay-<name>@<ver> from npm`, `unpacked to ./.relay/flows/<name>/`, `compiled flow.ts`, `validated flow definition against @relay/core`), summary line `installed in <duration>s.`, blank line, `try it:` and indented `relay run <name> .`. Resolve bare name → `@ganderbite/relay-<name>` (note: product spec uses the `relay-<name>` convention over `flow-<name>`; follow the product spec). Run `npm install --no-save --prefix ./.relay/flows/<flow> <packageSpec>`. Flatten the nested `node_modules/@ganderbite/relay-<name>/` directory to `./.relay/flows/<flow>/`. Cache catalog registry at `~/.relay/registry.json` with 1h TTL. Import visuals from task_77.

waves.name: relay search
waves.description: Create `packages/cli/src/commands/search.ts`. Output matches product spec §6.8 `relay search` example: header `●─▶●─▶●─▶●  search: <query>`, blank line, one row per match: `<name padded>  v<ver>  <dur>  $<cost>    <tier>` where tier is `verified` (gray) or `community` (dim). Per §6.8 the tier badge is the trust signal. Fetch `https://relay.dev/registry.json` with 5s timeout, filter by query substring-matching on name/displayName/tags/description. Sort: verified first, then installCount desc (if available in registry). Footer: `<N> matches. install with: relay install <name>`. On network failure: `⚠ unable to reach catalog; retry later.` (yellow). Use cache from task_54. Import visuals from task_77.

waves.name: relay upgrade
waves.description: Create `packages/cli/src/commands/upgrade.ts`. With no arg: iterate every dir in `./.relay/flows/*/` and call the install command handler for each with the same name (picks up latest respecting the original semver range in package.json). With `<flow>` arg: upgrade just that flow. Print a per-flow diff of version strings before/after.
